title: Browser Approval Submission First-Row Execution Gate Dispatch
objective: Prepare the strict future-executor dispatch packet and copy/paste prompt for the first browser approval submission and first hosted approval-row creation, while this coordinator lane performs no live POST, creates no approval row, and keeps project import blocked.
active_role: Technical Repo Authority / PM Lane Coordinator / Live Approval Gate Dispatch Author

source_floor.meaning: Current clean-main after PM Lane 141 authored the browser approval submission design bridge without live POST authority.

allowed_scope: Author a future-executor packet, handoff, and copy/paste prompt for the first browser approval submission lane.
Require an explicit stakeholder admission phrase before the future executor may send a live approval POST or create the first approval row.

Define future execution phases for local UI implementation, mocked browser validation, hosted deployment if needed, live first-row submission, idempotent replay proof, approval-status readback, and unchanged downstream domain-count proof.
Preserve project import, workpackage, task, apparatus, assignment, schedule, status, field records, production tracking, workbook, and AI business-state writes as blocked.
Update PM operating docs and status surfaces so the next relay step is repo-visible.
not_allowed: No live POST to /api/v1/mutations/project-import-approvals in this coordinator lane.
No approval row creation in this coordinator lane.
No browser approval button or product code change in this coordinator lane.
No project, workpackage, task, apparatus, issue, assignment, schedule, status, durable field record, production tracking, workbook, or import rows.
No live Supabase SQL application or schema migration.
No Render, Vercel, Olares, or Supabase action.
No secret value access, print, rotation, or repo storage.
No workbook macro execution or workbook writeback.
No service creation, DNS/auth/ingress change, fixture replay into live data, work authorization, field release, live work order creation, or autonomous AI business-state mutation.

explicit_admission_required_for_future_e.required_phrase: I explicitly admit PM Lane 142 live approval POST and first approval-row creation for the current Project Miner Temp Power import candidate.
explicit_admission_required_for_future_e.stop_rule: If the future executor prompt does not include the exact required phrase from Jason or the coordinator, the executor must stop after local mocked validation and must not send a live POST, deploy, or create an approval row.
explicit_admission_required_for_future_e.admitted_write_when_present: Exactly one browser approval persistence row may be created through POST /api/v1/mutations/project-import-approvals for the current candidate, followed by same-payload idempotent replay proof. No import mutation is admitted.

requirements: Confirm PM Lane 141 packet and handoff are present.
Confirm hosted PM intake reads, approval-status GET, and approval POST OpenAPI registration are green using existing smokes.
Confirm current approval record count for the candidate is zero before first-row execution.
Edit only operations-web import-intake UI and focused smoke files if a browser control does not yet exist.
Use the PM Lane 141 envelope and payload contract exactly.
Mock mutation calls in local Playwright proof; do not allow unmocked /api/v1/mutations calls during local smoke.
Deploy only the existing operations-web Vercel project if UI changes are required.
Use only the existing production alias https://operations.apexpowerops.com.
Do not create new Vercel, Render, Supabase, or Olares services.
Use the browser approval submission path, not a direct database write.
Send exactly one live POST for the current candidate and decision context.
Verify response status accepted, entity_type pm_import_candidate_approval, action_type persist_import_approval, and new_state.import_authority not_admitted.
Run same-payload idempotent replay proof and verify it does not create a second approval row.
Run approval-status readback and verify expected candidate classification.
Verify project, workpackage, task, apparatus, assignment, schedule, status, durable field record, and production tracking counts are unchanged.

future_acceptance_evidence: Exact source commit and deployed commit when hosted UI deployment is required.
Local mocked Playwright approval-submission proof with zero unmocked API calls.
Hosted route and PM intake smoke proof after any operations-web promotion.
Pre-submit approval record count for the candidate.
Live POST response proof with secret-free payload summary.
Idempotent replay proof showing one approval record.
Explicit guardrail confirmation that project import remains blocked.
validation_steps: rg -n "PM Lane 142|explicitly admit PM Lane 142|No live POST|No approval row|first approval-row|project import remains blocked" PROJECT_STATUS.md docs/operations ops/agents
validation_result: PASS: packet JSON parsed, executor copy/paste prompt path exists, guardrail search confirms PM Lane 142/no-live-write language across status/docs/artifacts, no unresolved placeholders remain in the Lane 142 packet or handoff, and scoped git diff check passed.
sidecar_result: Read-only sidecar agreed PM Lane 142 should be dispatch/packet/prompt only, not live execution. It recommended touching only status/docs plus the three Lane 142 artifacts, keeping all live permissions false, and requiring the exact explicit admission phrase before any live approval POST or first approval-row creation.
next_recommended_lane: PM Lane 142A local mocked browser approval UI implementation, or PM Lane 142 live first-row execution only after the exact explicit admission phrase is provided
